introduction: Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is any degree of glucose intolerance first recognised in pregnancy. If inadequately managed, GDM is associated with increased risk of perinatal morbidity and mortality. Diagnosis and prompt institution of therapy reduce the risks of poor outcomes.

features: Women with high risk for GDM

investigations: Daily SBGM (urine glucose monitoring) is not useful in pregnancy
Regular assessment of maternal wellbeing should include blood pressure and urine protein

non_drug: Combined health care team including obstetrician, diabetologist, diabetes educator, and paediatrician/neonatologist
Spread carbohydrate over 3 small to moderate sized meals and 2 - 3 snacks/day
Consider an evening snack to prevent starvation ketosis
Energy intake should provide for desirable weight gain during pregnancy
For obese women a 30 - 33% calorie restriction is advised
Withdraw therapy for diabetes after birth
Re-assess classification of maternal status at 6 weeks post partum
drug: Initiate insulin therapy if fasting plasma glucose is > 5.8 mmol/L, 1 hour post-prandial glucose is > 8.6 mmol/L, 2 hour post-prandial plasma glucose is >7.5 mmol/L
Modify insulin regimen to achieve above targets

complications: Acute metabolic complications of diabetes mellitus
